text.title: agentic-rag-tool-using Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for agentic-rag-tool-using. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Cap the tool-using loop at max_calls (recommended 3). Each loop iteration MUST log the tool name, input query, and a truncated result summary for offline debugging.
rule.rationale: Without a cap, a confused tool selector can loop indefinitely. Without logging, tool selection decisions are a black box that is impossible to debug in production.

rule.statement: The tool description passed to the LLM MUST be specific enough to distinguish tools: "vector_search: semantic similarity search in document database" vs "keyword_search: exact keyword matching for precise terms" — not just tool names.
rule.rationale: Ambiguous tool descriptions cause the LLM to default to the first tool in the list regardless of query type, defeating the purpose of the registry.

rule.statement: Use fast/cheap models (Haiku or gpt-4o-mini) for tool routing. Reserve Opus or gpt-4o for final answer generation. Separate the routing budget from the synthesis budget.
rule.rationale: Tool routing is a classification task (which tool?) that does not require deep reasoning. Routing with Opus burns ~10x the budget for no quality gain on the routing decision itself.

rule.statement: Cache tool results within a single agentic run. If the same tool is called with the same query in a later iteration, return the cached result without re-executing the tool.
rule.rationale: The same sub-query can recur across iterations; re-executing the tool wastes latency and API cost without adding new information.
